html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Seventeen — Part Five

The Book of Yesha'yahu

YESHA'YAHU

The Book of Isaiah — 1QIsa-a Great Isaiah Scroll

Chapters 55 through 66

Come Everyone Who Thirsts — The New Creation — All Flesh Shall Come to Worship

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

1QIsa-a — The Great Isaiah Scroll — Cave 1 Qumran — COMPLETE

The oldest and most complete prophetic manuscript in existence

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

YESHA'YAHU — CHAPTERS 55 THROUGH 66 — THE NEW CREATION AND THE COVENANT CONSUMMATION

YESHA'YAHU — CHAPTER 55 Come Everyone Who Thirsts — Seek YHWH While He May Be Found — My Word Shall Not Return Empty

1 Come, everyone who thirsts, come to the waters. And he who has no money, come, buy and eat. Come, buy wine and milk without money and without price.

2 Why do you spend your money for that which is not bread, and your labor for that which does not satisfy? Listen diligently to me, and eat what is good, and delight yourselves in rich food.

3 Incline your ear, and come to me. Hear, that your soul may live. And I will make with you an everlasting covenant, my steadfast, sure love for Dawid.

4 Behold, I made him a witness to the peoples, a leader and commander for the peoples.

5 Behold, you shall call a nation that you do not know, and a nation that did not know you shall run to you, because of YHWH your Creator, and of the Holy One of Yisra'EL, for he has glorified you.

6 Seek YHWH while he may be found — call upon him while he is near.

7 Let the wicked forsake his way, and the unrighteous man his thoughts. Let him return to YHWH, that he may have compassion on him, and to our Creator, for he will abundantly pardon.

8 For my thoughts are not your thoughts, neither are your ways my ways, declares YHWH.

9 For as the heavens are higher than the earth, so are my ways higher than your ways and my thoughts than your thoughts.

10 For as the rain and the snow come down from heaven and do not return there but water the earth, making it bring forth and sprout, giving seed to the sower and bread to the eater,

11 So shall my word be that goes out from my mouth — it shall not return to me empty, but it shall accomplish that which I purpose, and shall succeed in the thing for which I sent it.

12 For you shall go out in joy and be led forth in peace. The mountains and the hills before you shall break forth into singing, and all the trees of the field shall clap their hands.

13 Instead of the thorn shall come up the cypress — instead of the brier shall come up the myrtle. And it shall make a name for YHWH, an everlasting sign that shall not be cut off.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QIsa-a preserves Chapter 55 in full. Verse 11 — my word shall not return to me empty, but it shall accomplish that which I purpose — is the covenant declaration of prophetic reliability that grounds the entire book of Yesha'yahu. The word that went out through Yesha'yahu — judgment and restoration — will accomplish its covenant purpose.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 55 in full. Verse 1-3 — come, everyone who thirsts, come to the waters — without money and without price — I will make with you an everlasting covenant, my steadfast sure love for Dawid — is read as the covenant invitation that extends the Davidic covenant to the entire exiled community.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 1QIsa-a and the Masoretic Text are in close agreement for Chapter 55. Verse 6-7 — seek YHWH while he may be found — let the wicked forsake his way — let him return to YHWH, for he will abundantly pardon. The covenant call to return is the hinge between the Servant Songs of Chapters 42-54 and the covenant community exhortations of Chapters 56-66.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 8-11 — my thoughts are not your thoughts — as the heavens are higher than the earth, so are my ways higher than your ways — so shall my word be — it shall not return empty. The covenant ground of prophetic confidence: YHWH's ways exceed human ways by the same measure as heaven exceeds earth. The prophetic word operates by covenant necessity — it goes out from YHWH's mouth and must return with accomplishment. The covenant word is not advice or suggestion — it is the declaration of the one whose ways are as high above human ways as heaven above earth.

YESHA'YAHU — CHAPTER 56 Maintain Justice — Keep the Sabbath — The Foreigner and the Eunuch — My House Shall Be Called a House of Prayer for All Peoples

1 Thus says YHWH: Keep justice, and do righteousness, for soon my salvation will come, and my righteousness be revealed.

2 Blessed is the man who does this, and the son of man who holds it fast, who keeps the Sabbath, not profaning it, and keeps his hand from doing any evil.

3 Let not the foreigner who has joined himself to YHWH say, YHWH will surely separate me from his people. And let not the eunuch say, Behold, I am a dry tree.

4 For thus says YHWH: To the eunuchs who keep my Sabbaths, who choose the things that please me and hold fast my covenant,

5 I will give in my house and within my walls a monument and a name better than sons and daughters. I will give them an everlasting name that shall not be cut off.

6 And the foreigners who join themselves to YHWH, to minister to him, to love the name of YHWH, and to be his servants, everyone who keeps the Sabbath and does not profane it, and holds fast my covenant —

7 These I will bring to my holy mountain, and make them joyful in my house of prayer. Their burnt offerings and their sacrifices will be accepted on my altar — for my house shall be called a house of prayer for all peoples.

8 The Lord YHWH, who gathers the outcasts of Yisra'EL, declares: I will gather yet others to him besides those already gathered.

9 All you beasts of the field, come to devour — all you beasts in the forest.

10 His watchmen are blind — they are all without knowledge. They are all silent dogs — they cannot bark. Dreaming, lying down, loving to slumber.

11 The dogs have a mighty appetite — they never have enough. But they are shepherds who have no understanding. They have all turned to their own way, each to his own gain, one and all.

12 Come, I will get wine — let us fill ourselves with strong drink. And tomorrow will be like today, great beyond measure.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QIsa-a preserves Chapter 56 in full. Verse 7 — my house shall be called a house of prayer for all peoples — is the covenant universalism that frames the final section of Yesha'yahu: Chapters 56-66. The foreigners and eunuchs who were excluded from the covenant assembly in Devarim are now included in the covenant community through covenant faithfulness.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 56 in full. Verse 3-5 — let not the foreigner say YHWH will separate me — let not the eunuch say I am a dry tree — for I will give them an everlasting name — is read in the Ethiopian tradition as the covenant expansion that explicitly includes the African nations within YHWH's covenant household.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 1QIsa-a and the Masoretic Text are in close agreement for Chapter 56. Verse 8 — I will gather yet others to him besides those already gathered. The covenant gathering is not limited to the return of the exiled covenant community — it includes nations not previously counted among the covenant people.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 7 — my house shall be called a house of prayer for all peoples. The covenant theological climax of the universalism that runs through the second half of Yesha'yahu: the temple — the specific covenant dwelling of YHWH's name in Yerushalayim — is declared the covenant destination for all peoples. The covenant community's exclusive covenant access is opened to all who join themselves to YHWH through covenant faithfulness — Sabbath keeping and covenant holding.

YESHA'YAHU — CHAPTER 57 The Righteous Man Perishes — Idolatry Condemned — Revive the Spirit of the Humble — There Is No Peace for the Wicked

1 The righteous man perishes, and no one lays it to heart. Devout men are taken away, while no one understands. For the righteous man is taken away from calamity.

2 He enters into peace — they rest in their beds who walk in their uprightness.

3 But you, draw near, sons of the sorceress, offspring of the adulterer and the loose woman.

4 Whom are you mocking? Against whom do you open your mouth wide and stick out your tongue? Are you not children of transgression, the offspring of deceit,

5 You who burn with lust among the oaks, under every green tree, who slaughter your children in the valleys, under the clefts of the rocks?

6 Among the smooth stones of the valley is your portion — they, they are your lot. To them you have poured out a drink offering — you have brought a grain offering. Shall I relent for these things?

7 On a high and lofty mountain you have set your bed, and there you went up to offer sacrifice.

8 Behind the door and the doorpost you have set up your memorial. For, deserting me, you have uncovered your bed — you have gone up to it — you have made it wide. And you have made a covenant with them — you have loved their bed — you have looked on nakedness.

9 You journeyed to the king with oil and multiplied your perfumes. You sent your envoys far off, and sent down even to Sheol.

10 You were wearied with the length of your way, but you did not say, It is hopeless. You found new life for your strength, and so you were not faint.

11 Whom did you dread and fear, so that you lied, and did not remember me, did not lay it to heart? Have I not held my peace, even for a long time, and you do not fear me?

12 I will declare your righteousness and your deeds, but they will not profit you.

13 When you cry out, let your collection of idols deliver you. The wind will carry them all off — a breath will take them away. But he who takes refuge in me shall possess the land and shall inherit my holy mountain.

14 And it shall be said, Build up, build up, prepare the way, remove every obstruction from my people's way.

15 For thus says the One who is high and lifted up, who inhabits eternity, whose name is Holy: I dwell in the high and holy place, and also with him who is of a contrite and lowly spirit, to revive the spirit of the lowly, and to revive the heart of the contrite.

16 For I will not contend forever, nor will I always be angry — for the spirit would grow faint before me, and the breath of life that I made.

17 Because of the iniquity of his unjust gain I was angry — I struck him. I hid and was angry, but he went on backsliding in the way of his own heart.

18 I have seen his ways, but I will heal him. I will lead him and restore comfort to him and his mourners,

19 Creating the fruit of the lips. Peace, peace, to the far and to the near, says YHWH, and I will heal him.

20 But the wicked are like the tossing sea, for it cannot be quiet, and its waters toss up mire and dirt.

21 There is no peace, says my Creator, for the wicked.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QIsa-a preserves Chapter 57 in full. Verse 15 — I dwell in the high and holy place, and also with him who is of a contrite and lowly spirit — is the covenant declaration of YHWH's dwelling: simultaneously in the transcendent heights and with the humble. The same YHWH who fills the throne vision of Chapter 6 dwells with the contrite.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 57 in full. Verse 19 — peace, peace, to the far and to the near, says YHWH, and I will heal him — is the covenant declaration of shalom offered to both the distant nations and the near covenant community simultaneously. The covenant peace is not geographically restricted.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 1QIsa-a and the Masoretic Text are in close agreement for Chapter 57. Verse 21 — there is no peace for the wicked — repeats the covenant boundary declaration of Chapter 48:22, forming the covenant bracket around the central servant songs and covenant promises.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 15 — I dwell in the high and holy place, and also with him who is of a contrite and lowly spirit, to revive the spirit of the lowly, to revive the heart of the contrite. The covenant paradox of YHWH's dwelling: the Creator who inhabits eternity and the heights simultaneously inhabits the contrite heart. The most exalted and the most crushed are both YHWH's dwelling places. The covenant community in exile — humiliated, contrite, lowly — is not abandoned but inhabited by YHWH.

YESHA'YAHU — CHAPTER 58 Is Not This the Fast That I Choose — Loose the Bonds of Wickedness — If You Turn Back Your Foot From the Sabbath

1 Cry aloud, spare not. Lift up your voice like a trumpet. Declare to my people their transgression, to the house of Yaakov their sins.

2 Yet they seek me daily and delight to know my ways, as if they were a nation that did righteousness and did not forsake the judgment of their Creator. They ask of me righteous judgments — they delight to draw near to Creator.

3 Why have we fasted, and you see it not? Why have we humbled ourselves, and you take no knowledge of it? Behold, in the day of your fast you seek your own pleasure, and oppress all your workers.

4 Behold, you fast only to quarrel and to fight and to hit with a wicked fist. Fasting like yours this day will not make your voice to be heard on high.

5 Is such the fast that I choose, a day for a person to humble himself? Is it to bow down his head like a reed, and to spread sackcloth and ashes under him? Will you call this a fast, and a day acceptable to YHWH?

6 Is not this the fast that I choose: to loose the bonds of wickedness, to undo the straps of the yoke, to let the oppressed go free, and to break every yoke?

7 Is it not to share your bread with the hungry and bring the homeless poor into your house — when you see the naked, to cover him, and not to hide yourself from your own flesh?

8 Then shall your light break forth like the dawn, and your healing shall spring up speedily. Your righteousness shall go before you — the glory of YHWH shall be your rear guard.

9 Then you shall call, and YHWH will answer. You shall cry, and he will say, Here I am. If you take away the yoke from your midst, the pointing of the finger, and speaking wickedness,

10 If you pour yourself out for the hungry and satisfy the desire of the afflicted, then shall your light rise in the darkness and your gloom be as the noonday.

11 And YHWH will guide you continually and satisfy your desire in scorched places and make your bones strong. And you shall be like a watered garden, like a spring of water, whose waters do not fail.

12 And your ancient ruins shall be rebuilt. You shall raise up the foundations of many generations. You shall be called the repairer of the breach, the restorer of streets to dwell in.

13 If you turn back your foot from the Sabbath, from doing your pleasure on my holy day, and call the Sabbath a delight and the holy day of YHWH honorable — if you honor it, not going your own ways, or seeking your own pleasure, or talking idly —

14 Then you shall take delight in YHWH, and I will make you ride on the heights of the earth. I will feed you with the heritage of Yaakov your father, for the mouth of YHWH has spoken.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QIsa-a preserves Chapter 58 in full. The covenant critique of ritual fasting disconnected from covenant justice — verse 3-7 — is the most comprehensive treatment of the relationship between covenant ritual and covenant ethics in the second half of Yesha'yahu. The fast YHWH chooses — verse 6-7 — is entirely social and relational: loose the bonds, free the oppressed, share bread, house the homeless, cover the naked.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 58 in full. Verse 6-7 — is not this the fast that I choose: to loose the bonds of wickedness, to undo the yoke, to let the oppressed go free, to share your bread with the hungry — is the covenant definition of acceptable worship: not cultic performance but covenant justice.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 1QIsa-a and the Masoretic Text are in close agreement for Chapter 58. Verse 12 — you shall be called the repairer of the breach, the restorer of streets to dwell in — is the covenant identity given to the community that practices the covenant fast: not merely recipients of restoration but active covenant restorers.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 3-5 — why have we fasted and you see it not? The covenant complaint mirrors the complaint of Chapter 1:15 — when you make many prayers I will not listen. The covenant community performs religious acts — fasting, humiliation — and receives no divine response. YHWH's diagnosis: the fast is performed while seeking your own pleasure and oppressing your workers. The religious performance is real. The covenant orientation is absent. Verse 6-7 — is not this the fast I choose: to loose the bonds, free the oppressed, share bread, house the poor. The covenant definition of true fasting is covenant action toward the most vulnerable.

YESHA'YAHU — CHAPTER 59 Your Iniquities Have Separated You From Your Creator — Truth Has Stumbled in the Broad Places — The Redeemer Shall Come to Tziyon

1 Behold, the hand of YHWH is not shortened, that it cannot save, or his ear dull, that it cannot hear.

2 But your iniquities have made a separation between you and your Creator, and your sins have hidden his face from you so that he does not hear.

3 For your hands are defiled with blood and your fingers with iniquity. Your lips have spoken lies — your tongue mutters wickedness.

4 No one enters suit justly — no one goes to law honestly. They rely on empty pleas — they speak lies. They conceive mischief and give birth to iniquity.

5 They hatch adder's eggs and weave the spider's web. He who eats their eggs dies, and from one that is crushed a viper is hatched.

6 Their webs will not serve as clothing — men will not cover themselves with what they make. Their works are works of iniquity, and deeds of violence are in their hands.

7 Their feet run to evil, and they are swift to shed innocent blood. Their thoughts are thoughts of iniquity — desolation and destruction are in their highways.

8 The way of peace they do not know, and there is no justice in their paths. They have made their roads crooked — no one who treads on them knows peace.

9 Therefore justice is far from us, and righteousness does not overtake us. We hope for light, and behold, darkness — and for brightness, but we walk in gloom.

10 We grope for the wall like the blind — we grope like those who have no eyes. We stumble at noon as in the twilight — among those in full vigor we are like dead men.

11 We all growl like bears — we moan and moan like doves. We hope for justice, but there is none — for salvation, but it is far from us.

12 For our transgressions are multiplied before you, and our sins testify against us. For our transgressions are with us, and we know our iniquities:

13 Transgressing, and denying YHWH, and turning back from following our Creator, speaking oppression and revolt, conceiving and uttering from the heart lying words.

14 Justice is turned back, and righteousness stands far away — for truth has stumbled in the broad places, and uprightness cannot enter.

15 Truth is lacking, and he who departs from evil makes himself a prey. YHWH saw it, and it displeased him that there was no justice.

16 He saw that there was no man, and wondered that there was no one to intercede. Then his own arm brought him salvation, and his righteousness upheld him.

17 He put on righteousness as a breastplate, and a helmet of salvation on his head. He put on garments of vengeance for clothing, and wrapped himself in zeal as a cloak.

18 According to their deeds, so will he repay — wrath to his adversaries, repayment to his enemies. To the coastlands he will render repayment.

19 So they shall fear the name of YHWH from the west, and his glory from the rising of the sun — for he will come like a rushing stream, which the wind of YHWH drives.

20 And a Redeemer will come to Tziyon, to those in Yaakov who turn from transgression, declares YHWH.

21 And as for me, this is my covenant with them, says YHWH: My Spirit that is upon you, and my words that I have put in your mouth, shall not depart out of your mouth, or out of the mouth of your offspring, or out of the mouth of your children's offspring, says YHWH, from this time forth and forevermore.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QIsa-a preserves Chapter 59 in full. Verse 21 — my Spirit that is upon you, and my words that I have put in your mouth, shall not depart out of your mouth, or out of the mouth of your offspring — forevermore — is the covenant declaration of the perpetual covenant of Spirit and word: the two covenant gifts of Chapter 32:15 and Chapter 55:11 combined into a permanent covenant inheritance.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 59 in full. Verse 2 — your iniquities have made a separation between you and your Creator, and your sins have hidden his face from you so that he does not hear — is read as the covenant diagnosis of prayer ineffectiveness: not YHWH's distance but covenant separation caused by iniquity.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 1QIsa-a and the Masoretic Text are in close agreement for Chapter 59. Verse 16-17 — YHWH saw that there was no man and no one to intercede — then his own arm brought salvation — he put on righteousness as a breastplate and a helmet of salvation. The covenant warrior imagery: YHWH arms himself with covenant attributes because no human arm could achieve the covenant purpose.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 14-16 — truth has stumbled in the broad places — truth is lacking — YHWH saw it and it displeased him that there was no justice — he saw that there was no man, and wondered that there was no one to intercede. The covenant crisis: the entire human covenant community has failed. Truth has fallen. No one intercedes. YHWH's response is not abandonment but direct covenant action: his own arm brings salvation. The covenant that depended on human covenant faithfulness is now carried by YHWH's own arm and righteousness.

YESHA'YAHU — CHAPTER 60 Arise Shine For Your Light Has Come — Nations Shall Come to Your Light — The Sun Shall No More Be Your Light

1 Arise, shine, for your light has come, and the glory of YHWH has risen upon you.

2 For behold, darkness shall cover the earth, and thick darkness the peoples. But YHWH will arise upon you, and his glory will be seen upon you.

3 And nations shall come to your light, and kings to the brightness of your rising.

4 Lift up your eyes all around, and see — they all gather together, they come to you. Your sons shall come from afar, and your daughters shall be carried on the hip.

5 Then you shall see and be radiant — your heart shall thrill and exult, because the abundance of the sea shall be turned to you, the wealth of the nations shall come to you.

6 A multitude of camels shall cover you, the young camels of Midyan and Efah — all those from Sheva shall come. They shall bring gold and frankincense, and shall bring good news, the praises of YHWH.

7 All the flocks of Kedar shall be gathered to you — the rams of Nevayot shall minister to you. They shall come up with acceptance on my altar, and I will beautify my beautiful house.

8 Who are these that fly like a cloud, and like doves to their windows?

9 For the coastlands shall hope for me, the ships of Tarshish first, to bring your children from afar, their silver and gold with them, for the name of YHWH your Creator, and for the Holy One of Yisra'EL, because he has made you beautiful.

10 Foreigners shall build up your walls, and their kings shall minister to you — for in my wrath I struck you, but in my favor I have had mercy on you.

11 Your gates shall be open continually — day and night they shall not be shut, that people may bring to you the wealth of the nations, with their kings led in procession.

12 For the nation and kingdom that will not serve you shall perish — those nations shall be utterly laid waste.

13 The glory of Lebanon shall come to you, the cypress, the plane, and the pine, to beautify the place of my sanctuary, and I will make the place of my feet glorious.

14 The sons of those who afflicted you shall come bending low to you, and all who despised you shall bow down at your feet. They shall call you the City of YHWH, the Tziyon of the Holy One of Yisra'EL.

15 Whereas you have been forsaken and hated, with no one passing through, I will make you majestic forever, a joy from age to age.

16 You shall drink the milk of nations — you shall nurse at the breast of kings. And you shall know that I, YHWH, am your Savior and your Redeemer, the Mighty One of Yaakov.

17 Instead of bronze I will bring gold, and instead of iron I will bring silver. Instead of wood, bronze, instead of stones, iron. I will make your overseers peace and your taskmasters righteousness.

18 Violence shall no more be heard in your land, devastation or destruction within your borders. You shall call your walls Salvation, and your gates Praise.

19 The sun shall be no more your light by day, nor for brightness shall the moon give you light. But YHWH will be your everlasting light, and your Creator will be your glory.

20 Your sun shall no more go down, nor your moon withdraw itself — for YHWH will be your everlasting light, and your days of mourning shall be ended.

21 Your people shall all be righteous — they shall possess the land forever, the branch of my planting, the work of my hands, that I might be glorified.

22 The least one shall become a clan, and the smallest one a mighty nation. I am YHWH — in its time I will hasten it.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QIsa-a preserves Chapter 60 in full. Chapter 60 is the covenant vision of Yerushalayim's glory restored and exceeded: the nations stream to Tziyon's light, their wealth and kings brought in procession, YHWH himself replacing sun and moon as the everlasting light. The covenant restoration exceeds the covenant creation.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 60 in full. Verse 6 — the young camels of Midyan and Efah, all those from Sheva shall come — they shall bring gold and frankincense — is read in the Ethiopian tradition as the covenant inclusion of the Afro-Asiatic nations in the covenant procession to Tziyon.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 1QIsa-a and the Masoretic Text are in close agreement for Chapter 60. Verse 19-20 — YHWH will be your everlasting light — your days of mourning shall be ended. The covenant replacement of the created luminaries with YHWH himself as the source of light is the covenant restoration of the pre-creation state described in Bereshit 1:3-5 — light existed before the sun was created.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1-3 — arise, shine, for your light has come — darkness shall cover the earth and thick darkness the peoples — but YHWH will arise upon you and his glory will be seen upon you — and nations shall come to your light. The covenant contrast: universal darkness and YHWH's covenant light on Tziyon simultaneously. The covenant community does not arise into neutral conditions but into a world of darkness — which makes the covenant light more, not less, visible. Verse 21 — your people shall all be righteous. The covenant community's transformation is total: not most, not many — all.

YESHA'YAHU — CHAPTER 61 The Spirit of YHWH Is Upon Me — To Proclaim the Year of YHWH's Favor — They Shall Be Called Oaks of Righteousness

1 The Spirit of the Lord YHWH is upon me, because YHWH has anointed me to bring good news to the poor. He has sent me to bind up the brokenhearted, to proclaim liberty to the captives, and the opening of the prison to those who are bound.

2 To proclaim the year of YHWH's favor, and the day of vengeance of our Creator — to comfort all who mourn —

3 To grant to those who mourn in Tziyon — to give them a beautiful headdress instead of ashes, the oil of gladness instead of mourning, the garment of praise instead of a faint spirit. They shall be called oaks of righteousness, the planting of YHWH, that he may be glorified.

4 They shall build up the ancient ruins — they shall raise up the former devastations. They shall repair the ruined cities, the devastations of many generations.

5 Strangers shall stand and tend your flocks — foreigners shall be your plowmen and vinedressers.

6 But you shall be called the priests of YHWH — they shall speak of you as the ministers of our Creator. You shall eat the wealth of the nations, and in their glory you shall boast.

7 Instead of your shame there shall be a double portion — instead of dishonor they shall rejoice in their lot. Therefore in their land they shall possess a double portion — they shall have everlasting joy.

8 For I YHWH love justice — I hate robbery and wrong. I will faithfully give them their recompense, and I will make an everlasting covenant with them.

9 Their offspring shall be known among the nations, and their descendants in the midst of the peoples. All who see them shall acknowledge them, that they are an offspring YHWH has blessed.

10 I will greatly rejoice in YHWH — my soul shall exult in my Creator. For he has clothed me with the garments of salvation — he has covered me with the robe of righteousness, as a bridegroom decks himself like a priest with a beautiful headdress, and as a bride adorns herself with her jewels.

11 For as the earth brings forth its sprouts, and as a garden causes what is sown in it to sprout up, so the Lord YHWH will cause righteousness and praise to sprout up before all the nations.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QIsa-a preserves Chapter 61 in full. Verse 1-2 — the Spirit of YHWH is upon me, because YHWH has anointed me to bring good news to the poor — is the fifth and final servant declaration in Yesha'yahu, connecting the anointed servant directly to the Spirit-poured-out covenant of Chapter 32:15 and 44:3.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 61 in full. Verse 6 — you shall be called the priests of YHWH — they shall speak of you as the ministers of our Creator — is read in the Ethiopian tradition as the covenant elevation of the entire covenant community to priestly status, fulfilling the promise of Shemot 19:6 — you shall be a kingdom of priests.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 1QIsa-a and the Masoretic Text are in close agreement for Chapter 61. Verse 3 — a beautiful headdress instead of ashes, oil of gladness instead of mourning, garment of praise instead of a faint spirit. The covenant triple reversal: each element of exile grief is replaced by its covenant counterpart.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1-2 — the Spirit of YHWH is upon me, because YHWH has anointed me to bring good news to the poor — to proclaim liberty to the captives — to proclaim the year of YHWH's favor. The covenant anointing is for covenant service to the most marginalized: the poor, the brokenhearted, the captive, the bound, those who mourn. The year of YHWH's favor — the Yovel year — is the covenant calendar reset that cancels debts, frees slaves, and restores land. The anointed servant's mission is the proclamation of cosmic Yovel: the covenant reset of all broken relationships.

YESHA'YAHU — CHAPTER 62 For Tziyon's Sake I Will Not Keep Silent — You Shall Be Called Hephzibah — Prepare the Way — The Redeemed of YHWH

1 For Tziyon's sake I will not keep silent, and for Yerushalayim's sake I will not be quiet, until her righteousness goes forth as brightness, and her salvation as a burning torch.

2 The nations shall see your righteousness, and all the kings your glory, and you shall be called by a new name that the mouth of YHWH will give.

3 You shall be a crown of beauty in the hand of YHWH, and a royal diadem in the hand of your Creator.

4 You shall no more be termed Forsaken, and your land shall no more be termed Desolate. But you shall be called Hephzibah, and your land Beulah, for YHWH delights in you, and your land shall be married.

5 For as a young man marries a young woman, so shall your sons marry you, and as the bridegroom rejoices over the bride, so shall your Creator rejoice over you.

6 On your walls, O Yerushalayim, I have set watchmen. All the day and all the night they shall never be silent. You who put YHWH in remembrance, take no rest,

7 And give him no rest until he establishes and makes Yerushalayim a praise in the earth.

8 YHWH has sworn by his right hand and by his mighty arm: I will not again give your grain to be food for your enemies, and foreigners shall not drink your wine for which you have labored.

9 But those who garner it shall eat it and praise YHWH, and those who gather it shall drink it in the courts of my sanctuary.

10 Go through, go through the gates — prepare the way for the people. Build up, build up the highway — clear it of stones. Lift up a signal over the peoples.

11 Behold, YHWH has proclaimed to the end of the earth: Say to the daughter of Tziyon, Behold, your salvation comes. Behold, his reward is with him, and his recompense before him.

12 And they shall be called The Holy People, The Redeemed of YHWH. And you shall be called Sought Out, A City Not Forsaken.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QIsa-a preserves Chapter 62 in full. The covenant name changes of verse 4 — Forsaken becomes Hephzibah — My Delight Is in Her — and Desolate becomes Beulah — Married — are the covenant renaming that reverses the exile. The covenant name change is the covenant identity transformation: what you are called is what you are in YHWH's covenant declaration.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 62 in full. Verse 5 — as the bridegroom rejoices over the bride, so shall your Creator rejoice over you — is read as the covenant marriage restoration: the divorce threatened in Chapter 50:1 that never happened is replaced by the bridegroom's active covenant rejoicing.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 1QIsa-a and the Masoretic Text are in close agreement for Chapter 62. Verse 6-7 — I have set watchmen on your walls who shall never be silent day or night — you who put YHWH in remembrance, give him no rest until he makes Yerushalayim a praise. The covenant intercessory watchmen as a permanent covenant institution.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 12 — they shall be called The Holy People, The Redeemed of YHWH. And you shall be called Sought Out, A City Not Forsaken. The four covenant names at the end of Chapter 62 are the covenant counter-declarations to the covenant accusations of Chapter 1: the sinful nation — now The Holy People. The covenant people who forsook YHWH — now The Redeemed of YHWH. The city that became a whore — now Sought Out. The desolate city — now A City Not Forsaken. The covenant arc of the entire book is compressed into four final covenant names.

THE COVENANT LAMENT AND PRAYER — CHAPTERS 63 THROUGH 64

YESHA'YAHU — CHAPTER 63 Who Is This Coming From Edom — The Day of Vengeance — YHWH's Steadfast Love — Where Is Your Zeal?

1 Who is this who comes from Edom, in crimsoned garments from Botzrah, he who is splendid in his apparel, marching in the greatness of his strength? It is I, speaking in righteousness, mighty to save.

2 Why is your apparel red, and your garments like his who treads in the wine press?

3 I have trodden the winepress alone, and from the peoples no one was with me. I trod them in my anger and trampled them in my wrath. Their lifeblood spattered on my garments, and stained all my apparel.

4 For the day of vengeance was in my heart, and my year of redemption had come.

5 I looked, but there was no one to help — I was appalled, but there was no one to uphold. So my own arm brought me salvation, and my wrath upheld me.

6 I trampled down the peoples in my anger — I made them drunk in my wrath, and I poured out their lifeblood on the earth.

7 I will recount the steadfast love of YHWH, the praises of YHWH, according to all that YHWH has granted us, and the great goodness to the house of Yisra'EL that he has granted them according to his compassion, according to the abundance of his steadfast love.

8 For he said, Surely they are my people, children who will not deal falsely. And he became their Savior.

9 In all their affliction he was afflicted, and the angel of his presence saved them. In his love and in his pity he redeemed them. He lifted them up and carried them all the days of old.

10 But they rebelled and grieved his Holy Spirit. Therefore he turned to be their enemy, and himself fought against them.

11 Then he remembered the days of old, of Moshe and his people. Where is he who brought them up out of the sea with the shepherds of his flock? Where is he who put in the midst of them his Holy Spirit,

12 Who caused his glorious arm to go at the right hand of Moshe, who divided the waters before them to make for himself an everlasting name,

13 Who led them through the depths? Like a horse in the desert, they did not stumble.

14 Like livestock that go down into the valley, the Spirit of YHWH gave them rest. So you led your people, to make for yourself a glorious name.

15 Look down from heaven and see, from your holy and beautiful habitation. Where are your zeal and your might? The stirring of your inner parts and your compassion are held back from me.

16 For you are our Father, though Avraham does not know us and Yisra'EL does not acknowledge us. You, O YHWH, are our Father — our Redeemer from of old is your name.

17 O YHWH, why do you make us wander from your ways and harden our heart, so that we fear you not? Return for the sake of your servants, the tribes of your heritage.

18 Your holy people held possession for a little while — our adversaries have trampled down your sanctuary.

19 We have become like those over whom you have never ruled, like those who are not called by your name.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QIsa-a preserves Chapter 63 in full. The divine warrior vision of verses 1-6 — YHWH returning from Edom with bloodied garments from the winepress of judgment — is the covenant military imagery of the one who acts alone when no human help is available. Verse 5 echoes Chapter 59:16 — no man to help, so my own arm brought salvation.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 63 in full. Verse 9 — in all their affliction he was afflicted — the angel of his presence saved them — in his love and pity he redeemed them — he lifted them up and carried them all the days of old — is read as the covenant declaration of YHWH's empathetic participation in the covenant community's suffering: their affliction becomes his affliction.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 1QIsa-a and the Masoretic Text are in close agreement for Chapter 63. Verse 16 — you are our Father, though Avraham does not know us and Yisra'EL does not acknowledge us. You, O YHWH, are our Father — our Redeemer from of old is your name. The covenant community appeals to YHWH as Father beyond the human patriarchs: even if Avraham and Yaakov disowned us, you are our Father.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 10 — they rebelled and grieved his Holy Spirit. The covenant grief of YHWH's Spirit is the covenant consequence of the people's rebellion. YHWH's Spirit is not impassive — it is grieved. The consequence: YHWH himself turned to be their enemy and fought against them. The one who carried them — verse 9 — became their adversary. The covenant relationship is dynamic, responsive, and costly in both directions.

YESHA'YAHU — CHAPTER 64 Oh That You Would Rend the Heavens — We Have All Become Like One Who Is Unclean — We Are the Clay You Are the Potter

1 Oh that you would rend the heavens and come down, that the mountains might quake at your presence —

2 As when fire kindles brushwood and the fire causes water to boil — to make your name known to your adversaries, and that the nations might tremble at your presence.

3 When you did awesome things that we did not look for, you came down — the mountains quaked at your presence.

4 From of old no one has heard or perceived by the ear, no eye has seen a Creator besides you, who acts for those who wait for him.

5 You meet him who joyfully works righteousness, those who remember you in your ways. Behold, you were angry, and we sinned — in our sins we have been a long time, and shall we be saved?

6 We have all become like one who is unclean, and all our righteous deeds are like a polluted garment. We all fade like a leaf, and our iniquities, like the wind, take us away.

7 There is no one who calls upon your name, who rouses himself to take hold of you — for you have hidden your face from us, and have made us melt in the hand of our iniquities.

8 But now, O YHWH, you are our Father. We are the clay, and you are our potter — we are all the work of your hand.

9 Be not so terribly angry, O YHWH, and remember not iniquity forever. Behold, please look, we are all your people.

10 Your holy cities have become a wilderness. Tziyon has become a wilderness, Yerushalayim a desolation.

11 Our holy and beautiful house, where our fathers praised you, has been burned by fire, and all our pleasant places have become ruins.

12 Will you restrain yourself at these things, O YHWH? Will you keep silent, and afflict us so terribly?

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QIsa-a preserves Chapter 64 in full. The covenant lament of Chapter 64 — paired with Chapter 63 — is the covenant prayer of the exiled community confronting the ruins of the temple: our holy and beautiful house has been burned — will you keep silent and afflict us so terribly? The covenant prayer moves from the vision of divine judgment — Chapter 63 — to the covenant petition for divine intervention.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 64 in full. Verse 8 — but now, O YHWH, you are our Father. We are the clay, and you are our potter — we are all the work of your hand — is read as the covenant surrender: the covenant community abandons every claim to righteousness — verse 6, all our righteous deeds are like a polluted garment — and appeals to the creator-creature covenant relationship.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 1QIsa-a and the Masoretic Text are in close agreement for Chapter 64. Verse 6 — all our righteous deeds are like a polluted garment. The covenant confession of the lament: even the covenant community's righteous acts are covenant-insufficient. The covenant ground of appeal is not merit but the creator-creature relationship of verse 8.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 4 — from of old no one has heard or perceived by the ear, no eye has seen a Creator besides you, who acts for those who wait for him. The covenant declaration of YHWH's uniqueness in the lament: the prayer is addressed to the one who alone acts on behalf of those who wait. Verse 8-9 — we are the clay, you are our potter — we are all the work of your hand — be not so terribly angry — behold, we are all your people. The covenant argument of the lament: as the potter does not destroy what his hands have made, so YHWH should not destroy the covenant people who are the work of his hands.

THE NEW CREATION — CHAPTERS 65 THROUGH 66 — ALL FLESH SHALL COME TO WORSHIP

YESHA'YAHU — CHAPTER 65 I Was Ready to Be Sought — Behold My Servants Shall Eat — The New Creation — The Wolf and Lamb Together

1 I was ready to be sought by those who did not ask for me. I was ready to be found by those who did not seek me. I said, Here I am, here I am, to a nation that was not called by my name.

2 I spread out my hands all the day to a rebellious people, who walk in a way that is not good, following their own devices,

3 A people who provoke me to my face continually, sacrificing in gardens and making offerings on bricks,

4 Who sit in tombs, and spend the night in secret places. Who eat pig's flesh, and broth of tainted meat is in their vessels.

5 Who say, Keep to yourself, do not come near me, for I am too holy for you. These are a smoke in my nostrils, a fire that burns all the day.

6 Behold, it is written before me. I will not keep silent, but I will repay — I will indeed repay into their lap

7 Both your iniquities and your fathers' iniquities together, says YHWH, because they made offerings on the mountains and insulted me on the hills. I will measure their former work into their lap.

8 Thus says YHWH: As the new wine is found in the cluster, and they say, Do not destroy it, for there is a blessing in it, so I will do for my servants' sake, and not destroy them all.

9 I will bring forth offspring from Yaakov, and from Yehudah inheritors of my mountains. My chosen shall inherit it, and my servants shall dwell there.

10 Sharon shall become a pasture for flocks, and the Valley of Achor a place for herds to lie down, for my people who have sought me.

11 But you who forsake YHWH, who forget my holy mountain, who set a table for Fortune and fill cups of mixed wine for Destiny —

12 I will destine you to the sword, and all of you shall bow down to the slaughter, because, when I called, you did not answer. When I spoke, you did not listen, but you did what was evil in my eyes and chose what I did not delight in.

13 Therefore thus says the Lord YHWH: Behold, my servants shall eat, but you shall be hungry. Behold, my servants shall drink, but you shall be thirsty. Behold, my servants shall rejoice, but you shall be put to shame.

14 Behold, my servants shall sing for gladness of heart, but you shall cry out for pain of heart and shall wail for breaking of spirit.

15 You shall leave your name to my chosen for a curse, and the Lord YHWH will put you to death. But his servants he will call by another name,

16 So that he who blesses himself in the land shall bless himself by the Creator of truth, and he who takes an oath in the land shall swear by the Creator of truth. Because the former troubles are forgotten and are hidden from my eyes.

17 For behold, I create new heavens and a new earth, and the former things shall not be remembered or come into mind.

18 But be glad and rejoice forever in that which I create — for behold, I create Yerushalayim to be a joy, and her people to be a gladness.

19 I will rejoice in Yerushalayim and be glad in my people. No more shall be heard in it the sound of weeping and the cry of distress.

20 No more shall there be in it an infant who lives but a few days, or an old man who does not fill out his days, for the young man shall die a hundred years old, and the sinner a hundred years old shall be accursed.

21 They shall build houses and inhabit them. They shall plant vineyards and eat their fruit.

22 They shall not build and another inhabit. They shall not plant and another eat. For like the days of a tree shall the days of my people be, and my chosen shall long enjoy the work of their hands.

23 They shall not labor in vain or bear children for calamity, for they shall be the offspring of the blessed of YHWH, and their descendants with them.

24 Before they call I will answer — while they are yet speaking I will hear.

25 The wolf and the lamb shall graze together — the lion shall eat straw like the ox, and dust shall be the serpent's food. They shall not hurt or destroy in all my holy mountain, says YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QIsa-a preserves Chapter 65 in full. Verse 17 — for behold, I create new heavens and a new earth — is the covenant declaration of cosmic renewal that responds to the covenant lament of Chapter 64. The covenant answer to the ruins of Tziyon and the burned temple is not restoration to the former state but creation of a covenant new creation.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 65 in full. Verse 24 — before they call I will answer — while they are yet speaking I will hear — is read as the covenant declaration of YHWH's prevenient covenant response: the prayer is answered before it is completed. The covenant intimacy of the new creation surpasses the covenant access of the old.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 1QIsa-a and the Masoretic Text are in close agreement for Chapter 65. Verse 25 — the wolf and the lamb shall graze together — repeats the covenant creation vision of Chapter 11:6-9. The covenant new creation restores the covenant harmony of the garden. The serpent's food shall be dust — the covenant reversal of the curse of Bereshit 3:14.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 17 — I create new heavens and a new earth — and the former things shall not be remembered or come into mind. The covenant declaration does not promise the restoration of what was but the creation of what has never been. The covenant new creation is not Eden restored — it is covenant life at a qualitatively higher level. Verse 24 — before they call I will answer. The covenant prayer relationship of the new creation: YHWH's response precedes the prayer. The covenant community that waited — Chapter 8:17, 26:8, 40:31 — will no longer need to wait. The covenant responsiveness of YHWH will be immediate and prevenient.

YESHA'YAHU — CHAPTER 66 Heaven Is My Throne — To This One I Will Look — YHWH Comes With Fire — All Flesh Shall Come to Worship

1 Thus says YHWH: Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What is the house that you would build for me, and what is the place of my rest?

2 All these things my hand has made, and so all these things came to be, declares YHWH. But this is the one to whom I will look: he who is humble and contrite in spirit and trembles at my word.

3 He who slaughters an ox is like one who kills a man — he who sacrifices a lamb, like one who breaks a dog's neck — he who presents a grain offering, like one who offers pig's blood — he who makes a memorial offering of frankincense, like one who blesses an idol. These have chosen their own ways, and their soul delights in their abominations.

4 I also will choose harsh treatment for them and bring their fears upon them, because when I called, no one answered — when I spoke, they did not listen. But they did what was evil in my eyes and chose what I did not delight in.

5 Hear the word of YHWH, you who tremble at his word: Your brothers who hate you and cast you out for my name's sake have said, Let YHWH be glorified, that we may see your joy — but it is they who shall be put to shame.

6 The sound of an uproar from the city — a sound from the temple — the sound of YHWH, rendering recompense to his enemies.

7 Before she was in labor she gave birth — before her pain came upon her she delivered a son.

8 Who has heard such a thing? Who has seen such things? Shall a land be born in one day? Shall a nation be brought forth in one moment? For as soon as Tziyon was in labor she brought forth her children.

9 Shall I bring to the point of birth and not cause to bring forth? says YHWH. Shall I, who cause to bring forth, shut the womb? says your Creator.

10 Rejoice with Yerushalayim, and be glad for her, all you who love her. Rejoice with her in joy, all you who mourn over her,

11 That you may nurse and be satisfied from her consoling breast — that you may drink deeply with delight from her glorious abundance.

12 For thus says YHWH: Behold, I will extend peace to her like a river, and the glory of the nations like an overflowing stream. And you shall nurse — you shall be carried upon her hip, and bounced upon her knees.

13 As one whom his mother comforts, so I will comfort you — you shall be comforted in Yerushalayim.

14 You shall see, and your heart shall rejoice — your bones shall flourish like the grass. And the hand of YHWH shall be known to his servants, and he shall be indignant toward his enemies.

15 For behold, YHWH will come in fire, and his chariots like the whirlwind, to render his anger in fury, and his rebuke with flames of fire.

16 For by fire will YHWH enter into judgment, and by his sword, with all flesh, and those slain by YHWH shall be many.

17 Those who sanctify and purify themselves to go into the gardens, following one in the midst, eating pig's flesh and the abomination and mice, shall come to an end together, declares YHWH.

18 For I know their works and their thoughts, and the time is coming to gather all nations and tongues. And they shall come and shall see my glory.

19 And I will set a sign among them. And from them I will send survivors to the nations, to Tarshish, Pul, and Lud, who draw the bow, to Tuval and Yavan, to the coastlands far away, that have not heard my fame or seen my glory. And they shall declare my glory among the nations.

20 And they shall bring all your brothers from all the nations as an offering to YHWH, on horses and in chariots and in litters and on mules and on dromedaries, to my holy mountain Yerushalayim, says YHWH, just as the Yisra'ELites bring their grain offering in a clean vessel to the house of YHWH.

21 And some of them also I will take for priests and for Levites, says YHWH.

22 For as the new heavens and the new earth that I make shall remain before me, says YHWH, so shall your offspring and your name remain.

23 From new moon to new moon, and from Sabbath to Sabbath, all flesh shall come to worship before me, declares YHWH.

24 And they shall go out and look on the dead bodies of the men who have rebelled against me. For their worm shall not die, their fire shall not be quenched, and they shall be an abhorrence to all flesh.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QIsa-a preserves Chapter 66 in full, completing the Great Isaiah Scroll. The final chapter of Yesha'yahu closes with the covenant vision of universal worship: all flesh shall come to worship before YHWH from new moon to new moon and Sabbath to Sabbath — and the permanent covenant distinction between those who worship and those who rebelled.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 66 in full. Verse 21 — some of them also I will take for priests and for Levites, says YHWH — is read in the Ethiopian tradition as the covenant inclusion of the nations in the priestly service: foreigners brought to YHWH's holy mountain are not merely worshipers but covenant ministers.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 1QIsa-a and the Masoretic Text are in close agreement for Chapter 66. Verse 23 — from new moon to new moon and from Sabbath to Sabbath, all flesh shall come to worship before me. The covenant worship calendar of the new creation is the covenant calendar of Yisra'EL — new moons and Sabbaths — extended to all flesh.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1-2 — heaven is my throne and the earth is my footstool — what is the house you would build for me? But this is the one to whom I will look: he who is humble and contrite in spirit and trembles at my word. The final covenant definition of covenant acceptability: not the temple, not the sacrifice, not the ritual — but the humble, contrite spirit that trembles at YHWH's word. The book that opened with YHWH's rejection of vain sacrifice — Chapter 1:11-15 — closes with YHWH's declaration of what he actually looks for: the trembling heart. Verse 23 — all flesh shall come to worship. The covenant arc of Yesha'yahu is complete: from the indictment of one sinful nation to the worship of all flesh before YHWH. The covenant scope of YHWH's purpose — declared in Chapter 2:2-4 — is finally and permanently established.

END OF PART FIVE — CHAPTERS 55 THROUGH 66 — YESHA'YAHU COMPLETE
